steps: Log in to your FullStory account at https://app.fullstory.com
Click the settings gear icon or navigate to Settings in the top navigation
Go to 'Integrations' in the left sidebar, then select 'API Keys'
Click 'Create key' in the top-right corner
Enter a meaningful key name (e.g., 'Gateway MCP')
Select the minimum required permissions for your use case
A modal displays the full API key value — copy it immediately, as it will not be shown again
Encode the key in Base64 and pass it as the Authorization header using the 'Basic' scheme

credential_fields.name: api_key
credential_fields.label: API Key
credential_fields.description: A FullStory API key from Settings > Integrations > API Keys. Shown only once at creation. Used with Basic auth (Base64-encoded).

notes: The API key is shown only once at creation — copy it immediately and store it securely
FullStory uses HTTP Basic Auth: Base64-encode 'api_key:' (note the colon, no password) and set Authorization: Basic <encoded>
API access requires a Business or Enterprise plan — not available on the free tier
Rate limits apply per organization; check FullStory documentation for current limits
Admin-level keys are required for user deletion; read-only keys are sufficient for session and event queries
Revoking a key immediately invalidates all requests using that key

test_endpoint.description: List users in the FullStory organization. Verifies API key and permissions are correctly configured.
